description.value:
Feral World

Feral worlders hail from planets where civilisation has regressed to a primitive state. Life is a daily struggle for survival against predators, harsh environments, and rival tribes. These worlds breed tough, resourceful individuals who rely on instinct and raw physical prowess. Though they may lack sophistication, their resilience and savage determination make them invaluable assets to any Inquisitorial warband.

Homeworld Bonus

+Strength, +Toughness, –Influence

Homeworld Aptitude

Toughness

Wounds

9+1d5

Fate Threshold

2 (Emperor's Blessing 3+)

Starting Skills

Survival

Starting Talent

Choose one: Weapon Training (choice)

Homeworld Trait

The Old Ways: Feral worlders distrust advanced technology. A feral world character suffers a –10 penalty to Tech-Use and Fellowship Tests when dealing with unfamiliar advanced technology, but gains a +10 bonus to Survival Tests in natural environments.

specialAbilities.name: The Old Ways
specialAbilities.description: A feral world character suffers a –10 penalty to Tech-Use and Fellowship Tests when dealing with unfamiliar advanced technology, but gains a +10 bonus to Survival Tests in natural environments.

system.notes: Emperor's Blessing: On a roll of 3+ on 1d10, the character begins with an extra Fate Point.
